fault_hypothesis: The non-overlap check on line 69 uses non-strict inequalities (`<=`, `>=`), causing it to fail for intervals that share a single endpoint. It should use strict inequalities (`<`, `>`) to correctly identify these boundary cases as overlaps.

constraints.intent: The buggy overlap logic works correctly only when the intervals do not share an endpoint.
If the function determines there is no overlap, the intervals must be strictly disjoint.
As long as the loop continues, it implies no overlap has been found, so the candidate must be strictly disjoint from the current reservation.
